description: Design responsive layouts using breakpoint variants to apply different styles at different screen sizes

actors.name: Designer/Developer
actors.description: Creates responsive layouts by applying breakpoint variants

actors.name: CSS Generator
actors.description: Converts breakpoint variants to CSS media queries

fields.name: breakpoint_name
fields.label: Identifier for screen size tier (e.g., sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl)

fields.name: mobile_first_style
fields.label: Base style applied at smallest screen (no breakpoint prefix)

fields.name: tablet_override_style
fields.label: Style applied at tablet breakpoint (e.g., md:)

fields.name: desktop_override_style
fields.label: Style applied at desktop breakpoint (e.g., lg:)

mobile_first_approach: Default styles (no breakpoint) apply to smallest screens
Breakpoint variants (sm:, md:, lg:) add or override styles at larger screens
Each breakpoint inherits and can override previous breakpoint styles
Breakpoints are min-width based; styles apply at breakpoint and above
breakpoint_ordering: Breakpoints must be ordered from smallest to largest
Default breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px), 2xl (1536px)
Custom breakpoints can be added by extending theme.screens
cascade: CSS media queries are ordered by breakpoint size; larger breakpoints come last
Larger breakpoint styles override smaller breakpoint styles (CSS cascade)
Multiple variants on same utility combine; sm:md:flex is invalid (variant order)

given: Sets grid to single column: 'grid grid-cols-1'
At tablet size (md: 768px), change to 2-column layout
add_tablet_layout.result: CSS media query generated: @media (min-width: 768px) { .md\:grid-cols-2 { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr)); } }

override_at_desktop.result: Mobile (1 col) → Tablet (2 col) → Desktop (3 col) responsive cascade

given: Element should be hidden on mobile, visible on tablet+
hide_element_on_mobile.result: Element hidden at base (display: none), shown at md+ (display: block)

given: Project needs custom breakpoint at 900px for specific layout (sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl not sufficient)
custom_breakpoint.result: Custom breakpoint available; utilities like 'custom:flex' generate media query @media (min-width: 900px)

given: Padding should be small on mobile, larger on desktop
Designer wants layout relative to container, not viewport (container queries)
container_query_responsive.result: Layout adapts to container size instead of viewport; styles apply when container meets size threshold

given: Custom breakpoints configured in wrong order: lg (1024px), md (768px), sm (640px)
invalid_breakpoint_order.result: CSS cascade broken; smaller breakpoints may override larger breakpoints unexpectedly

errors.message: Breakpoints must be ordered from smallest to largest. Fix theme.screens to ensure correct cascade.
Breakpoint variant not recognized. Check breakpoint name in theme.screens config.

related.reason: Responsive variants (md:, lg:) apply utilities at breakpoints

best_practices: Start with mobile layout; progressively enhance for larger screens
Keep breakpoint threshold values consistent across project
Use named breakpoints (sm, md) not pixel values in markup
